mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an imminent economic contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and residential construction activity confirms a broad-based demand expansion, which historically precedes sustained corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline GDPC1-only model in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during confirmed demand expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production remains elevated above its historical trend, supply-side efficiency gains translate directly into higher corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 102, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward validation over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances indicate sustained household spending momentum, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling RSAFS periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% imposes restrictive financial conditions that compress equity multiples and delay capital expenditure cycles, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for CPIAUCSL trends.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong non-farm payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate signals a labor market transition phase where wage pressures outpace productivity, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model fit over a single-series baseline, or forward returns are not significantly lower during confirmed divergence periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases characterized by sub-3.0% inflation, accelerating housing starts act as a leading indicator for broader economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when HOUST accelerates during high-inflation periods, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
